reasoning: PoC builds the full system-contract environment at canonical SystemAddresses (Timestamp, all configs, DKG, Reconfiguration, PerformanceTracker, ValidatorManagement) plus a MockStaking (real pools unnecessary because the eviction path only reads getPoolVotingPower and a try/catch'd renewPoolLockup). 4 validators are bootstrapped directly to ACTIVE via ValidatorManagement.initialize(GenesisValidator[]) — the genesis path skips the BLS PoP precompile. With autoEvictEnabled=true and DKG variant=Off pre-set at genesis, two pranked calls from SystemAddresses.GOVERNANCE to Reconfiguration.governanceReconfigure() reproduce the exploit faithfully: call #1 is harmless (closingEpoch<=1 skip-guard) but resets PerformanceTracker to all-zero and bumps epoch to 2; call #2 then triggers evictUnderperformingValidators reading (0,0) for every validator, hits the unconditional total==0 branch, and onNewEpoch demotes every validator except the last to INACTIVE in the same tx. Forge PASS on first attempt, gas=540585; getActiveValidatorCount() collapses from 4 to 1, totalVotingPower drops from 400 ether to 100 ether, all on commit a623eab.
